title: Aechmea basilateralis
taxonomy: Aechmea basilateralis is classified within the family Bromeliaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as pineapples or air plants. This classification places Aechmea basilateralis alongside other bromeliads, such as the popular Aechmea fasciata. The family Bromeliaceae is further grouped into several subfamilies, including Tillandsioideae and Bromelioideae. Within the genus Aechmea, Aechmea basilateralis is a species that exhibits unique characteristics. By classification, Aechmea basilateralis is a type of epiphytic bromeliad.

intro: Aechmea basilateralis, commonly known as the silver vase bromeliad, is a type of epiphytic plant belonging to the family Bromeliaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, wounds, and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Aechmea basilateralis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Aechmea basilateralis is often prepared as a tea or decoction, or used in combination with other herbs to create infusions and tinctures for its therapeutic uses.
